Brisbane’s Trusted Roof Restoration Specialists

At WeatherpRoof Restorations, we’re not just fixing roofs — we’re protecting homes, families, and investments. Locally owned and operated, we’ve built a reputation across Brisbane for delivering honest advice, quality workmanship, and long-lasting results.

We specialise in full roof restorations, repairs, painting, and guttering — using only the highest quality materials, like Roof Protect coatings from Industrial Roof Coatings. Every job we take on is backed by real experience, attention to detail, and a commitment to doing things the right way — no shortcuts, no surprises.

With over 1,000 successful projects completed, we know what it takes to restore your roof to its best — and keep it that way.
Call Us Today!
OUR LOCATION

Roofing Experts In Brendale & Surrounding Suburbs

Proudly servicing Brendale, Strathpine, Warner, Albany Creek, Bald Hills, and Lawnton with top-quality roof restorations, repairs, and painting. Whether you’re dealing with leaks, worn-out tiles, or simply want a fresh new look, our experienced team delivers reliable results that stand up to Queensland’s tough weather.
FINANCE YOUR ROOF

Affordable Roofing Solutions With Easy Financing

Get the Roof You Need — Without the Stress
At Weatherproof Restorations, we believe that everyone deserves a strong, reliable roof — without the burden of upfront costs. That’s why we offer flexible financing options designed to make your roof restoration or repairs simple and affordable. There’s no credit check required to apply, approvals are fast and easy, and the entire process is completely hassle-free.
Call Us Today!

Frequently Asked Questions

Take a look at what are some of the most frequently asked questions by our customers, to find the answers you are looking for.

The cost of tile roof repairs can vary significantly depending on the scope of work required. Factors such as the extent of damage, the number of broken or slipped tiles, roof pitch and access, and whether additional work like valley replacements, ridge repointing, or waterproofing is needed will all influence the overall cost.

Every roof is different, which is why we offer tailored quotes based on a thorough inspection. This allows us to assess your roof properly and provide a clear outline of what needs to be done and the associated costs.

If you’d like us to take a look at your roof and provide a no-obligation quote, get in touch with our team today

The cost to restore a roof depends on a range of factors, including the size and pitch of the roof, its current condition, the type of roofing material, and the specific work required. A full roof restoration typically involves pressure cleaning, replacing broken tiles, re-bedding and repointing ridge capping, applying protective sealers, and repainting — but every roof is different.

Because no two jobs are the same, we provide tailored quotes based on a detailed roof inspection. This ensures you receive a clear scope of work and a fair, accurate price for your specific roof.

Ready to find out what your roof needs? Contact us for a free quote

All of our roofers are fully trade qualified and bring a wealth of hands-on experience to every job. As a team, we have over 100 years of combined roofing experience, covering everything from minor tile repairs to full roof restorations and complex problem-solving.

We pride ourselves on quality workmanship, attention to detail, and getting the job done right the first time. When you choose us, you’re choosing a team that knows roofs inside and out.

Have questions or want to book a quote? We’re here to help

We stand behind the quality of our work, which is why all of our roof restorations and repairs come with a written warranty for your peace of mind. The type and length of the warranty depends on the service provided, but generally:

  • Roof restorations come with both a workmanship warranty and a paint product warranty of 15 or 20 years — depending on whether or not the roof has been previously painted.
  • Roof repairs are backed by a workmanship guarantee covering the areas we’ve repaired.

Our warranties cover issues related to the work and materials we’ve supplied. If something isn’t right, we’ll come back to inspect it and make it right.

We take pride in delivering long-lasting, professional results — and our guarantees are a reflection of that commitment.

Have questions about your specific warranty? Get in touch

CONTACT US

Ready to chat? Get in touch today.

Servicing Brisbane Northside and up to Caboolture, Weatherproof Restorations offer an honest and reliable service for those looking for roof restorations, repairs, cleaning, leak detection, repointing, rebedding and solar panel cleaning.

## Affordable Roofing Solutions With Easy Financing

Get the Roof You Need — Without the Stress

At Weatherproof Restorations, we believe that everyone deserves a strong, reliable roof — without the burden of upfront costs. That’s why we offer flexible financing options designed to make your roof restoration or repairs simple and affordable. There’s no credit check required to apply, approvals are fast and easy, and the entire process is completely hassle-free.

[Call Us Today!](tel:0720000041)

## Frequently Asked Questions

Take a look at what are some of the most frequently asked questions by our customers, to find the answers you are looking for.

### How Much Do Tile Roof Repairs Cost?

The cost of tile roof repairs can vary significantly depending on the scope of work required. Factors such as the extent of damage, the number of broken or slipped tiles, roof pitch and access, and whether additional work like valley replacements, ridge repointing, or waterproofing is needed will all influence the overall cost.

Every roof is different, which is why we offer tailored quotes based on a thorough inspection. This allows us to assess your roof properly and provide a clear outline of what needs to be done and the associated costs.

If you’d like us to take a look at your roof and provide a no-obligation quote, [get in touch](https://weatherproof.net.au/contact-us/) with our team today

### How much will you charge for fixing or restoring my roof?

The cost to restore a roof depends on a range of factors, including the size and pitch of the roof, its current condition, the type of roofing material, and the specific work required. A full roof restoration typically involves pressure cleaning, replacing broken tiles, re-bedding and repointing ridge capping, applying protective sealers, and repainting — but every roof is different.

Because no two jobs are the same, we provide tailored quotes based on a detailed roof inspection. This ensures you receive a clear scope of work and a fair, accurate price for your specific roof.

Ready to find out what your roof needs? [Contact us](https://weatherproof.net.au/contact-us/) for a free quote

### How experienced are your roofing contractors?

All of our roofers are fully trade qualified and bring a wealth of hands-on experience to every job. As a team, we have over 100 years of combined roofing experience, covering everything from minor tile repairs to full roof restorations and complex problem-solving.

We pride ourselves on quality workmanship, attention to detail, and getting the job done right the first time. When you choose us, you’re choosing a team that knows roofs inside and out.

Have [questions or want to book a quote](https://weatherproof.net.au/contact-us/)? We’re here to help

### How does your guarantee/warranty work?

We stand behind the quality of our work, which is why all of our roof restorations and repairs come with a written warranty for your peace of mind. The type and length of the warranty depends on the service provided, but generally:

- **Roof restorations** come with both a workmanship warranty and a **paint product warranty of 15 or 20 years** — depending on whether or not the roof has been previously painted.
- **Roof repairs** are backed by a workmanship guarantee covering the areas we’ve repaired.

Our warranties cover issues related to the work and materials we’ve supplied. If something isn’t right, we’ll come back to inspect it and make it right.

We take pride in delivering long-lasting, professional results — and our guarantees are a reflection of that commitment.

Have questions about your specific warranty? [Get in touch](https://weatherproof.net.au/contact-us/)

ABOUT US

ABOUT US

We are dedicated to providing quality reliable roofing services.

Servicing Brisbane Northside and up to Caboolture, Weatherproof Restorations offer an honest and reliable service for those looking for roof restorations, repairs, cleaning, leak detection, repointing, rebedding and solar panel cleaning.

With over 25 years’ experience, Nick Parry started Weatherproof Restorations in 2018 and has been growing his qualified team and building a positive reputation for great customer service ever since.
Call Us Today!

Our Process

STEP 1

Roof Inspection
& Repairs

We start by assessing your roof for damage, identifying broken tiles, cracks, or structural issues. Any necessary repairs or repointing are completed to ensure a strong foundation.
STEP 1

High-Pressure
Cleaning

Using industrial high-pressure cleaners, we remove dirt, debris, mould, and flaking paint. This deep clean is essential for proper paint adhesion and long-lasting results.
STEP 1

Mould &
Fungus

An antifungal solution is applied to eliminate existing growth and prevent future mould or mildew—especially important in Queensland’s humid climate.
STEP 1

Primer & Sealing
Coat

We apply a high-quality primer and sealer to lock in the clean surface, improving paint grip and protecting your roof from moisture and heat.
STEP 5

Roof
Painting

We apply a high-quality primer and sealer to lock in the clean surface, improving paint grip and protecting your roof from moisture and heat.
ABOUT US

Why Choose WeatherpRoof Restorations?

At Weatherproof Restoration we know the importance of maintaining and taking care of what your roof needs. As one of the most expensive assets for your home or commercial site, your roof deserves to be treated as an investment. We set the benchmark for roofing by producing quality work for our customers, time and time again.

Our team works hard to complete the job right the first time with a professional and friendly attitude. We help to build an environment of trust with our customers and an open policy when it comes to what’s on our invoices. There is no job too big or too small for our team. We always ensure that we are approachable to our customers, and happy to answer any of your questions.
Call Us Today!
OUR VALUES

Honest, Trustworthy & Reliable

Honesty, trust and reliability are what Weatherproof Restorations pride ourselves on. Since beginning in 2018 we have gained a following of customers through our friendly service and quality work. Our qualified team look after our customers from quote to long after they have paid their invoice. We carry out free follow-up checks on our work to ensure that we leave you with the best results.

When it comes to value and reliability, Weatherproof Restorations are the only name you need to know.

Top Signs Your Roof Needs Immediate Attention 

Regular roof maintenance is essential to ensure your roof remains in good condition. The condition of your roof naturally impacts its functionality and strength. Taking good care of your roof ensures that it is strong and durable, able to protect against storms and is unlikely to collapse. Looking after your roof could be lifesaving.  

Long term disrepair, water and weather damage and neglect can cause roofs to buckle and break, leak or collapse. When roofs collapse, all those heavy roofing materials will undoubtedly fall inwards.  

Roof collapses are incredibly dangerous and can result in the death/s of the occupants. It can and does happen. Collapses can cause injury, severe property damage or death. 

The worse the roof is, the more likely it is to collapse. Hopefully, we’ve encouraged you to think about your roof and consider getting it inspected or having a look yourself at the very least. But what do you look for? 

 

Here are a few signs that your roof needs immediate attention, either maintenance or a full restoration:  

  • Sagging Roof 

A sagging roof is a big problem and requires immediate attention. This is not one of those “deal with it later” problems. A sagging roof means that something has gone wrong with the structural integrity of the roof or the supports. Roofing materials aren’t supposed to bend inward; they can only hold on for so long before they collapse.  

  • Missing or damaged roof tiles 

This issue requires less immediate attention but still needs to be attended to. Missing or damaged roof tiles allow weather and pests into your roof or your home. This will cause a multitude of problems for you in time.  

  • Clogged gutters 

Clogged gutters need to be unclogged regularly as water and debris will build up in the gutters and slowly lift roof tiles in your roof which will allow the water to sink in causing leaks and further lifting of tiles.  

  • Increased energy bills 

A sudden increase in energy costs or noticeable difference in the temperature indoors, implies there is a problem with your insulation. This is another issue that needs to be resolved as soon as possible as cracked or worn insulation means that not only does cool or warm air seep out, but weather can also get in too.  

  • Damaged flashing 

Flashing helps to direct water away from areas on your roof that may be slightly weaker like the area surrounding a skylight, for example. Without flashing water could pool there and eventually wear through the insulation causing a leak. Damaged flashing is the first step to leaks. 

  • Leaks and water stains 

Leaks will likely be the first-time people really notice and think about their roof. Leaks are persistent and frustrating, there is quite literally a hole in your roof. Water stains are a sign of leaks in your roof too. They discolour your roof over time and could require extensive repairs depending on the severity of the damage.  

 

WeatherpRoof Restorations is here to help. From simple maintenance and inspections to full service roof restorations, we can refresh your roof in no time!

All You Need to Know About Our Full Roof Restorations

A roof restoration is a full-service restoration that resolves any and all issues with your roof. From leaks and damaged flashing to cleaning and repairing or replacing damaged roof tiles and everything in between. This is different to a roof repair as the area being revitalised is much larger. While a roof repair might cover one specific issue, a restoration covers the entire area of your roof. Roof restorations are ideal for improving street appeal, increasing your property value and better weatherproofing your house to protect against the elements. 

Roof restorations are all about the long-term benefits, and are ideal for older roofs. This is because older roofs have had to weather many storms, figuratively and literally, which causes wear and tear over time. I often find that older roofs are also less maintained than newer ones as life happens and bigger purchases fall by the wayside. I understand though, a roof is not really something people think about until there’s a reason to, like a leak or water damage. 

A full roof restoration involves a variety of elements, we begin with a thorough inspection of your roof where we look for signs of leaks, damage or other issues so we know what we’re working with. Once we understand the unique issues your roof presents, we’ll move on to a rigorous cleaning. For this step, we do a deep pressure clean of the whole roof, and then we re-bed and re-point your roof to ensure your roof is properly sealed. Now comes any necessary fixes or repairs to refresh your roof. Lastly, the painting. We use a four-coat paint system with high-quality paint to keep your roof sparkling and of course, weatherproof for years to come.

Overall, when we’re done with a roof restoration it’s better than new! That’s always our goal and we see it through, time and time again. A full roof restoration is one of those you get what you pay for things, despite them still being cheaper than getting a whole new roof. Why Brisbane’s Climate Affects Your Roof More Than You Think

Brisbane’s climate is known for its hot summers, heavy rainfall, and impressive storms. But what does the climate have to do with your roof?

Well, because the roof is an exposed structure, it will inevitably be exposed to the weather. At all times. This impacts the state of your roof either over time from normal wear and tear or immediately from things like an overturned tree during a storm.

All of the weather characteristic to this area can take a serious toll on your roof. Many homeowners focus only on maintaining or attending to what they can see, leaving the roof to often become neglected. This is unfortunate as what is a house without a good roof? 

Understanding how Brisbane’s weather conditions impact your roof can help you be proactive about extending its lifespan and avoid costly repairs.

 

  1. Intense Sun and Heat

We’re all well aware of our long, hot summers and with temperatures often exceeding 30°C, and how they can damage, well anything really. This extreme heat causes the materials your roof is made up of, like roof tiles and metal sheets, to expand and contract. Over time, this back and forth can lead to cracks, warping, and weakened structural integrity of the materials themselves. UV exposure also degrades roofing sealants and coatings, making your roof more vulnerable to leaks. Overall, the heat baking down onto your roof can absolutely cause damage in the long term.

 

  1. Heavy Rain and Humidity

Brisbane is prone to sudden and extreme downpours, especially during our summer storm season. Bad drainage or clogged gutters can lead to water pooling on the roof and flowing beneath your shingles increasing the risk of leaks and mould growth. Excess moisture can also cause wooden structures to rot and sag, leading to further structural issues and eventual caving in of the roof.

 

  1. Severe Storms and Hail

Storm season is quite simply, savage. This period brings strong winds, freezing hail, and even cyclones, which can severely damage roofs. Storms are capable of destroying roofs all on their own, but severe storms often bring or create debris. Storms can upturn trees, logs, etc causing further risk to roofs. Flying debris can crack tiles, dent metal roofing, and even dislodge entire roof sections. Regular inspections and timely repairs can help prevent minor damage from escalating into major problems.

 

How to Protect Your Roof

Regular maintenance is step one! I cannot express how important regular maintenance is to ensure your roof remains in tip top shape.  This includes cleaning your gutter, regular roof inspections performed by an expert, and timely repairs. These are essential tasks for Brisbane homeowners to consider and either attend to themselves or hire someone to handle them. Choosing high-quality, weather-resistant roofing materials can also provide better protection against the elements. 

By staying proactive, you can ensure your roof withstands Brisbane’s harsh climate, keeping your home safe and secure year-round.

WeatherpRoof Restorations can help you with that!

9 Signs Your Roof Needs Repair

Regular roof maintenance is the gold standard for ensuring a roof is in good repair. This is the best way to make sure you avoid unfortunate and costly surprises. However, what happens if you’ve just moved into a house with a roof that has not been maintained or if you’ve simply let maintenance fall by the wayside?

Do you know what signs to look out for to know if your roof needs repairing?

Well, we’ve made you a list!

 

Here are nine of the most common signs your roof needs a repair:

  1. Missing Or Damaged Shingles

Missing or damaged shingles are common problems that require repair to avoid future damage as if left unattended can lead to leaks and costly structural damage. Shingles that are cracked or have curled edges may need to be replaced or they can let in moisture and vermin as they are no longer doing their job properly.  

  1. Water Damage 

Water damage is a big problem for roofs as it can cause a great many problems. Water damage can indicate your roof has a leak or is decaying. Water damage can result in ugly water spots and stains on ceilings that will need to be replaced. If water damage is left untreated, it will lead to mould and will ultimately get worse until chunks of your ceiling and/or roof could come falling into your home. 

  1. Sagging Roof 

A sagging roof is a big problem, it can be caused by storm damage or other external damage or just general wear and tear. A sagging roof can eventually lead to collapse if not dealt with quickly enough. Sagging often points to structural issues beneath the shingles which must be dealt with before the roof can be repaired.

  1. Increased Energy Bills 

High energy bills can be a sign that something is wrong with your roof. Roofs are made to maintain temperatures within the structure. If they’re not doing so, that’s a sign your roof is letting heat or cool air escape for some reason. This makes your heating and cooling systems work harder, which costs you more money. Increased energy bills are often one of the first ways that people discover their roof needs to be repaired. 

  1. Damaged Flashing 

Flashing refers to the material that seals gaps in roofs and walls. This is done to prevent water damage, and it can be concealed or exposed. Damaged flashing allows water to penetrate underneath and right into your home, which is a pretty big problem in our minds. 

Flashing can also simply age and weather over time and rust through. 

  1. Clogged Gutters

Clogged gutters may not seem like a big deal but they are. Gutters need to drain the water that comes off your roof and if they’re clogged with biological material, water will pool in the gutters which could damage the gutters, but also the water will eventually fill the gutter and flow beneath your shingles or sit and fester against your roof. Over time, this will cause water damage, and you can expect stinky stagnant water to pour into your nice cosy home. 

  1. Roof Age

Roofs, like anything, age and break down over time. As your roof gets older, normal wear and tear will occur. This is the ideal reason to get a roof inspection as time goes on. It may look perfectly fine, but it may be on the verge of crumbling. Hiring an expert to check it out is important. 

  1. Mould

Mould is so dangerous. It is absolutely, unequivocally not something you can ignore. Mould gives off microscopic spores that you can breathe in and depending on the type of mould growing on or in your roof, you can expect symptoms like decreased lung function, problems breathing, nosebleeds, memory loss or even vomiting blood.

WeatherpRoof Restorations can assist with any and all of these roofing issues in Brisbane. Give us a call to get your repairs started today.

Flashing can also simply age and weather over time and rust through.

7. Clogged Gutters

Clogged gutters may not seem like a big deal but they are. Gutters need to drain the water that comes off your roof and if they’re clogged with biological material, water will pool in the gutters which could damage the gutters, but also the water will eventually fill the gutter and flow beneath your shingles or sit and fester against your roof. Over time, this will cause water damage, and you can expect stinky stagnant water to pour into your nice cosy home.

8. Roof Age

Roofs, like anything, age and break down over time. As your roof gets older, normal wear and tear will occur. This is the ideal reason to get a roof inspection as time goes on. It may look perfectly fine, but it may be on the verge of crumbling. Hiring an expert to check it out is important.

9. Mould

Mould is so dangerous. It is absolutely, unequivocally not something you can ignore. Mould gives off microscopic spores that you can breathe in and depending on the type of mould growing on or in your roof, you can expect symptoms like decreased lung function, problems breathing, nosebleeds, memory loss or even vomiting blood.

[WeatherpRoof Restorations](https://weatherproof.net.au/) can assist with any and all of these roofing issues in Brisbane. Give us a call to get your repairs started today.

What Is a Green Roof & Are They a Good Idea?

Green roofs are also known as vegetated roofs or living roofs and essentially are roofs that are designed to support the growth of plants like grasses, mosses or various other lightweight plants that need very little upkeep, these are called intensive green roofs. Extensive green roofs are almost like gardens or parks and can support heavier plants like trees! These need regular maintenance. 

Green roofs are gaining favour with many because of their gorgeous aesthetic and their sustainability benefits. They also reduce water runoff, absorb heat and sound and offer a safe space for pollinators or local wildlife to eat from or live. This concept is also great for urban farming initiatives where space is limited but high-rise buildings are plentiful.

Should you cultivate a green roof though? 

They have many benefits, but they also have a great deal of negative aspects too. They’re expensive to set up, you can’t just pop some moss up on your roof and expect it to thrive with no other effort. You need to consider the drainage and build provisions for that. There also needs to be soil, insulation and safeguards to protect the roof itself. 

A lack of proper drainage can cause leaks, and it will be much more expensive if THAT is the reason your roof is leaking. I would not suggest extensive living roofs on anything other than commercial buildings as trying to plant a tree on top of a normal house’s roof is most likely going to end badly. 

Living roofs offer better air quality than normal roofs as they pump oxygen into the environment. They also absorb the surrounding carbon dioxide and other gases that contribute to climate change. But again, they’re much heavier than conventional roofs, which means you need stronger foundations and load-bearing walls. 

This is not the kind of thing that is simple to pop onto a pre-existing structure, and more so something you involve your architect in helping you develop. They will reduce electricity costs as they cool the area more and create shade. They offer a habitat for local wildlife to live in, which is wonderful, but do you really want them on your roof? I’m not sure your neighbours will be pleased.

Living roofs are really great, but definitely something to think long and hard about before developing one. 

WeatherpRoof Restorations focuses on maintaining, repairing and restoring conventional roofs. If you’re interested in having us, come out and have a look, get in touch!

Don’t DIY Your Roof Restoration! 

Restoring or doing repairs on your roof, can seem simple but costly, so along comes the thought “why not do it yourself?” Unless you are a qualified roofer, this is generally not a good idea as your roof protects your entire house, everything you own and your family from the elements. One tiny leak can cause long term problems, imagine was poorly attempted DIY could do.

Roof restorations include assessing existing or potential roof issues, deep pressure cleaning, re-bedding and repointing, and then a high-quality painting 4 coat system. This is not all they include however, and some things are more specialised than others. Cleaning and inspecting your roof will be more thorough and efficient if you let the professionals handle this, as they know exactly what to look for. They also have years of experience and can easily spot things that will become problems later. 

Personal safety is a major concern when dealing with a roof in general, particularly for those who are new to handling roof maintenance. Everything from climbing up the ladder and then traipsing around that high off the ground is dangerous. There are many steps involved for professional roofers to keep them safe on the job and knowing what you’re doing when you repair or restore a roof is key. There’s a lot more that goes into it than people think.

Did you know that warranties will become void if materials are not installed by a licensed professional? Often the warranties are half the reason for choosing specific materials in the first place. Don’t risk not being up to code causing potential issues later on. Avoid all of this by simply hiring a professional roof restoration expert.

We at WeatherpRoof Restorations are registered, licensed and insured to ensure everything goes according to plan. We know what to look for, how to maintain, repair or restore it, no matter what “it” is. With decades of combined experience in the industry, and a track record of great service and happy customers, we are the obvious choice!

Get in touch to get your roof back in tip top shape!

Planning To Sell? How’s Your Roof?

Are you planning to sell your home in the near future? One of the first things a potential buyer will notice is the state of the roof. As we all know, a poorly maintained roof can lower your asking price or even require repairs as a condition of the sale. The more neglected or damaged the roof, the higher the restoration cost. To get your desired price, the roof must be in good condition.

Leaks can damage ceilings, walls, floors, and upholstery with prolonged leaks could potentially lead to mould growth. Holes in the roof or broken tiles allow pests to enter and rain and debris to come in during storms. Regular roof maintenance is ideal for addressing any potential damage immediately. However, if maintenance has been neglected, an inspection is necessary to determine the level of repairs or restorations needed. It might not be as bad as you think; sometimes, a power wash and fresh paint can make a significant difference.

Cleaning out your gutters is also essential, as clogged gutters can cause problems regardless of the roof’s condition. If there are a few broken tiles and some mould, repairs or replacements will make the roof sale-ready.

A full roof restoration is the best option when selling your home. This process covers everything from fixing deep issues to replacing broken tiles, cleaning, removing mould and pests, and repainting the entire roof. A recently restored roof gives potential buyers peace of mind and makes them more eager to buy.

If you’ve neglected your roof maintenance, WeatherpRoof Restorations can assist! Our team collectively has over 250 years of experience, and your neighbours trust us. We’re fully licensed, insured, and the obvious choice. We offer comprehensive roof inspections to identify any issues and provide detailed estimates for the necessary repairs or restorations. Our skilled professionals use high-quality materials and the latest techniques to ensure your roof is in top condition.

We can get your roof in tip-top shape in no time! Give us a call, so we can help you get your price and ensure a smooth selling process. Let WeatherpRoof Restorations take the worry out of roof maintenance, so you can focus on selling your home with confidence.

# Planning To Sell? How’s Your Roof?

Are you planning to sell your home in the near future? One of the first things a potential buyer will notice is the state of the roof. As we all know, a poorly maintained roof can lower your asking price or even require repairs as a condition of the sale. The more neglected or damaged the roof, the higher the restoration cost. To get your desired price, the roof must be in good condition.

Leaks can damage ceilings, walls, floors, and upholstery with prolonged leaks could potentially lead to mould growth. Holes in the roof or broken tiles allow pests to enter and rain and debris to come in during storms. Regular roof maintenance is ideal for addressing any potential damage immediately. However, if maintenance has been neglected, an inspection is necessary to determine the level of repairs or restorations needed. It might not be as bad as you think; sometimes, a power wash and fresh paint can make a significant difference.

Cleaning out your gutters is also essential, as clogged gutters can cause problems regardless of the roof’s condition. If there are a few broken tiles and some mould, repairs or replacements will make the roof sale-ready.

A full roof restoration is the best option when selling your home. This process covers everything from fixing deep issues to replacing broken tiles, cleaning, removing mould and pests, and repainting the entire roof. A recently restored roof gives potential buyers peace of mind and makes them more eager to buy.

If you’ve neglected your roof maintenance, [WeatherpRoof Restorations](https://weatherproof.net.au/) can assist! Our team collectively has over 250 years of experience, and your neighbours trust us. We’re fully licensed, insured, and the obvious choice. We offer comprehensive roof inspections to identify any issues and provide detailed estimates for the necessary repairs or restorations. Our skilled professionals use high-quality materials and the latest techniques to ensure your roof is in top condition.

We can get your roof in tip-top shape in no time! Give us a call, so we can help you get your price and ensure a smooth selling process. Let WeatherpRoof Restorations take the worry out of roof maintenance, so you can focus on selling your home with confidence.
